meta.title: BHCPF National Benefit Package
meta.scope: National standard (BHCPF) - applies across all Nigerian states, including Plateau
core_rules: Enrollees receive these services free of charge; providers must not charge for anything in this package.
Primary care must be accessed at the enrollee's registered PHC facility.
Secondary care must be accessed via referral to the nearest General Hospital.
Complaints or requests for money by a provider can be reported via the BHCPF hotline: 07007001111.

services.details: Consultation and prescribed drugs from accredited PHC facilities.

Family planning (safe period, pills, condoms, etc.); dental health; HIV/AIDS/TB/Malaria education; immunization; Vitamin A supplementation; nutrition promotion for children and pregnant women; personal, domestic and environmental hygiene promotion.

Incision & drainage, suturing of lacerations, minor burns, simple abrasions, minor wound debridement, circumcision of male infants, evacuation of impacted faeces, relief of urinary retention, primary eye care (conjunctivitis, parasitic/allergic ailments, simple contusions/abrasions).

services.service: Child welfare and common childhood illness treatment
services.details: Growth monitoring, routine NPHCDA immunization, Vitamin A supplementation, nutrition advice; management of uncomplicated malnutrition; treatment of helminthiasis, malaria, diarrheal disease, schistosomiasis, URTIs, uncomplicated pneumonia, UTIs, otitis media, pharyngitis, childhood exanthemas, skin diseases/infestations, mumps; treatment of anaemia not requiring transfusion.
services.limits: Anaemia treatment excludes cases requiring blood transfusion (refer to secondary level).

services.service: Management of common adult infections and conditions
services.details: Malaria, respiratory infections, UTIs, gastroenteritis, ENT infections, diarrheal disease, enteritis/typhoid, schistosomiasis, helminthiasis, skin infections (chicken pox, fungal e.g. tinea); emergency management of bites/stings (excl. serum); simple anaemia (not requiring transfusion); screening & referral for diabetes/hypertension/chronic disease; simple arthritis/musculoskeletal issues; routine sickle cell disease management; allergies.
services.limits: Serum for bites/stings not included at primary level; diabetes/hypertension are screened and referred, not treated at primary level.

services.details: Voluntary counselling and testing for HIV/STDs.
Anxiety neurosis, psychosomatic illnesses, insomnia, identification of drug abuse.

services.service: Family planning, antenatal, postnatal and delivery services
services.details: Basic family planning commodities; routine antenatal clinic, drugs for duration of pregnancy, routine urine/blood tests, referral for complicated cases; postnatal care for all live births up to 6 weeks (cord care, eye care, management of simple neonatal infections); spontaneous vaginal delivery by skilled attendant incl. repair of birth injuries/episiotomy; essential drugs for emergency obstetric care (EmOC).
services.limits: Postnatal coverage capped at 6 weeks from birth.

services.details: Airway assessment/adjuncts, aspiration and clearance, breathing assessment (e.g. ambu-bag), pulse oximetry, bleeding control (compression dressing), haemodynamic assessment, IV line/venous cut-down, fluid resuscitation, basic CPR, assessment/management of unconscious patient, suturing of small lacerations (no resuscitation needed), splinting of fractures/cervical spine.
Malaria parasite test, urinalysis, HB/PCV, stool microscopy, urine microscopy, pregnancy test, blood glucose test.
Consultation and prescribed drugs from accredited secondary healthcare facility.
Coverage for emergencies occurring outside the enrollee's usual residence or accredited healthcare provider.
Inpatient admission coverage.
services.limits: Max 15 cumulative days/year for medical admissions; max 20 cumulative days/year for surgical admissions.

services.details: As defined in the HIV Treatment Protocol.

Severe respiratory/urinary infections, diarrhoea with moderate-severe dehydration, enteric fever, severe malaria, septicaemia, meningitis, severe measles; childhood non-communicable disease management; severe anaemia requiring transfusion; neonatal sepsis; birth asphyxia, neonatal jaundice, care for infants of diabetic mothers.

services.service: Moderate-severe infection and chronic disease management
services.details: Severe malaria, meningitis, septicaemia, complicated RTIs/typhoid; diabetes and hypertension management; sickle cell disease management; severe musculoskeletal conditions; cardiovascular, renal (nephritis, nephrotic syndrome), liver disease (hepatitis, amoebic liver abscess); severe anaemia; snake bites (including serum).

Comprehensive emergency obstetric care; P/PROM management; hypertensive disease in pregnancy; bleeding in pregnancy; postpartum haemorrhage; eclampsia; caesarian section; ectopic gestation surgery; intra-uterine fetal death management; puerperal sepsis; instrumental/high-risk deliveries (1st, 4th+, multiples, malposition); hysterectomy for uncontrolled PPH; laparotomy for gynae emergencies; myomectomy; infertility investigations.
Appendicectomy, herniorrhaphy, hydrocelectomy, management of testicular torsion, fracture management.
Simple and surgical tooth extraction for medical reasons without complications.
Major trauma, pterygium, glaucoma, cataract extraction, other simple ophthalmic surgery; foreign body removal; refraction incl. spectacles.
Antral wash-out, foreign body removal, tonsillectomy, nasal polypectomy, tracheostomy, adenoidectomy, myringotomy.
Post-traumatic rehabilitation; management of palsies; post-cerebrovascular accident therapy.
services.limits: Palsy and post-CVA therapy limited to 15 days with a maximum of 5 sessions each.

services.details: Genotype, lumbar puncture, urea/electrolyte/creatinine, liver function test, ketone bodies, microscopy/culture/sensitivity (urine, blood, stool, sputum, wound, urethral, ear, eye, throat, aspirate, CSF, endoscopy, cervical swab, HVS), occult blood in stool, skin snip for microfilaria, AFB for TB, gram stain, Mantoux test, blood grouping/cross-matching, Hepatitis B surface antibody screening, confirmatory HIV test, full blood count, platelet/reticulocyte count, blood transfusion, chest/abdomen/skull/extremity/dental X-ray, abdominopelvic & obstetric scan.
services.limits: Blood transfusion capped at up to 3 pints of safe whole blood or blood product(s).
